pattern: Putting the largest values first keeps every running total as high as possible for as long as possible, which is exactly what maximizes how many prefix sums stay positive.
hints: The order is entirely up to you, so think about which arrangement keeps the running sum as large as possible at every single step.
Sorting from largest to smallest front-loads the biggest gains, delaying the effect of any negative numbers for as long as possible and never wasting a large positive value by placing it too late.
Once sorted that way, simply walk the array left to right accumulating a running sum and count how many of those running totals are strictly greater than zero.

edge_inputs.description: all negative values, no arrangement can ever go positive
all positive values, every prefix counts regardless of order
a zero in the mix does not itself count as a positive prefix
one large positive value must be spent first to keep several smaller negatives from ever winning

stress.description: large random arrays across the full value range
